revenue.name: Total Revenue
revenue.description: Total sales revenue from all completed orders
revenue.sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(line_total), 0) as revenue FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed'

gross_profit.name: Gross Profit
gross_profit.description: Revenue minus cost of goods sold
gross_profit.sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(gross_profit), 0) as gross_profit FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed'

net_profit.name: Net Profit
net_profit.description: Revenue minus all expenses (COGS + operating expenses)
net_profit.sql: WITH revenue AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(line_total), 0) as total_revenue FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' ), cogs AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(quantity * cost_price), 0) as total_cogs FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' ), operating_expenses AS ( SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0) as total_expenses FROM fact_expenses ) SELECT (total_revenue - total_cogs - total_expenses) as net_profit FROM revenue, cogs, operating_expenses

cash_runway.name: Cash Runway
cash_runway.description: Number of months until cash runs out at current burn rate
cash_runway.sql: WITH monthly_burn AS ( SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(date AS DATE)) as month, SUM(amount) as monthly_expenses FROM fact_expenses GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(date AS DATE)) ORDER BY month DESC LIMIT 3 ), avg_monthly_burn AS ( SELECT AVG(monthly_expenses) as avg_burn FROM monthly_burn ), current_cash AS ( SELECT signed_amount as cash_balance FROM fact_cash_flow ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 1 ) SELECT ROUND(CAST(cash_balance / NULLIF(avg_burn, 0) AS numeric), 1) as cash_runway_months FROM current_cash, avg_monthly_burn

top_products.name: Top Selling Products
top_products.sql: SELECT dp.product_name, dp.category, SUM(fs.line_total) as revenue, SUM(fs.quantity) as units_sold FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_products dp ON fs.product_id = dp.product_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dp.product_id, dp.product_name, dp.category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

marketing_roi.name: Marketing ROI
marketing_roi.description: Return on investment for marketing campaigns
marketing_roi.sql: SELECT dc.campaign_name, dc.platform, SUM(fm.spend) as total_spend, SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) as attributed_revenue, ((SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) - SUM(fm.spend)) / SUM(fm.spend)) * 100 as roi_percentage FROM fact_marketing fm JOIN dim_campaigns dc ON fm.campaign_id = dc.campaign_id GROUP BY dc.campaign_id, dc.campaign_name, dc.platform HAVING SUM(fm.spend) > 0 ORDER BY roi_percentage DESC

avg_order_value.name: Average Order Value
avg_order_value.sql: SELECT AVG(line_total) as avg_order_value FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed'

revenue_by_segment.name: Revenue by Customer Segment
revenue_by_segment.description: Total revenue broken down by customer segment (Retail, SME, Corporate)
revenue_by_segment.sql: SELECT dc.segment, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.customer_id) as customers, ROUND(CAST(AVG(fs.line_total) AS numeric), 2) as avg_order_value FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dc.segment ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

revenue_by_channel.name: Revenue by Sales Channel
revenue_by_channel.description: Total revenue broken down by sales channel
revenue_by_channel.sql: SELECT channel, SUM(line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY channel ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

revenue_by_city.name: Revenue by City
revenue_by_city.description: Total revenue and orders broken down by customer city
revenue_by_city.sql: SELECT dc.city, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dc.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

expense_breakdown.name: Expense Breakdown by Category
expense_breakdown.sql: SELECT expense_category, SUM(amount) as total_amount, COUNT(*) as num_entries FROM fact_expenses GROUP BY expense_category ORDER BY total_amount DESC

top_customers.name: Top Customers by Revenue
top_customers.description: Customers ranked by total revenue generated
top_customers.sql: SELECT dc.full_name, dc.segment, dc.city, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as total_orders FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dc.customer_id, dc.full_name, dc.segment, dc.city ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

orders_by_city.name: Orders by City
orders_by_city.description: Number of orders and revenue from each city
orders_by_city.sql: SELECT dc.city, COUNT(DISTINCT fs.order_id) as num_orders, SUM(fs.line_total) as total_revenue FROM fact_sales fs JOIN dim_customers dc ON fs.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE fs.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dc.city ORDER BY num_orders DESC

worst_campaign.name: Worst Performing Campaigns
worst_campaign.description: Campaigns ranked by lowest ROI (wasting money, negative returns)
worst_campaign.sql: SELECT dc.campaign_name, dc.platform, SUM(fm.spend) as total_spend, SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) as attributed_revenue, ((SUM(fm.revenue_attributed) - SUM(fm.spend)) / NULLIF(SUM(fm.spend), 0)) * 100 as roi_percentage FROM fact_marketing fm JOIN dim_campaigns dc ON fm.campaign_id = dc.campaign_id GROUP BY dc.campaign_id, dc.campaign_name, dc.platform HAVING SUM(fm.spend) > 0 ORDER BY roi_percentage ASC
